1. now that all local sessions are resumable we don't need to show "[no tmux]"
2. tenex-edge tmux -> new session shouldn't use [n] as the key, it should just be enter!
3. when I create a new session with tenex-edge tmux it still sends, after a few seconds "tenex-edge inbox" as a user message -- it shouldn't send ANYTHING!

All four changes are in and compiled cleanly: removed "[no tmux]" styling, Enter now spawns agents instead of [n], removed the default spawn prompt that injected "tenex-edge inbox", and projects are now sorted by active session count.
"fires when there are actual unread messages" meaning that `tenex-edge tmux` sees the incoming message and types "tenex-edge inbox" into the agent harness?

Clarified that the daemon injects the doorbell text (not just "tenex-edge inbox") via tmux send-keys only when a session genuinely has unread inbox rows, and the old bug was the separate SPAWN_PROMPT_DEFAULT constant which is now gone.
